en.extract:
The Barusan cuckoo-dove (Macropygia modiglianii) is a species of bird in the family Columbidae. It is found on three of the western Sumatran islands.
The Barusan cuckoo-dove was formerly considered as a subspecies of the ruddy cuckoo-dove. It was promoted to species status based on the results of an acoustic study published in 2016.
Three subspecies are recognised, each endemic to a specific island:

M. m. modiglianii Salvadori, 1887 – Nias Island
M. m. elassa Oberholser, 1912 – Mentawai Islands
M. m. hypopercna Oberholser, 1912 – Simeulue Island

The '''Barusan cuckoo-dove''' ('''''Macropygia modiglianii''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Columbidae]]. It is found on three of the western [[Sumatra|Sumatran islands]].

The Barusan cuckoo-dove was formerly considered as a [[subspecies]] of the [[ruddy cuckoo-dove]]. It was promoted to species status based on the results of an acoustic study published in 2016.<ref>{{ cite journal | last1=Ng | first1= E.Y.X. | last2= Eaton | first2=J.A. |last3=Verbelen | first3=P. | last4=Hutchinson | first4=R.O. | last5=Rheindt | first5=F.E. | year=2016 | title=Using bioacoustic data to test species limits in an Indo-Pacific island radiation of ''Macropygia'' cuckoo doves | journal=Biological Journal of the Linnean Society | volume=118 | issue=4 | pages=786–812 | doi=10.1111/bij.12768 | doi-access=free }}</ref><ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela Rasmussen | year=2020 | title=Pigeons | work=IOC World Bird List Version 10.1 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/pigeons/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | accessdate=14 March 2020 }}</ref>
 
Three [[subspecies]] are recognised, each endemic to a specific island:<ref name=ioc/>
* ''M. m. modiglianii'' <small>[[Tommaso Salvadori|Salvadori]], 1887</small> – [[Nias|Nias Island]]
* ''M. m. elassa'' <small>[[Harry C. Oberholser|Oberholser]], 1912</small> – [[Mentawai Islands Regency|Mentawai Islands]]
* ''M. m. hypopercna'' <small>[[Harry C. Oberholser|Oberholser]], 1912</small> – [[Simeulue|Simeulue Island]]
